title: procedure-annotator/stimulus
description: JEAB Procedure category, stimulus sub-annotator. Declares the identity and function of stimuli used in the experimental procedure.

reinforcer.alias_note: All three collapse to the same AST node. The source keyword is preserved as a pragmatic hint (label field) but does not affect equivalence judgment.

brief.description: Second-order schedule brief stimulus. @brief(none) explicitly opts out (no conditioned reinforcer). Omitting @brief on SecondOrder triggers MISSING_BRIEF warning (constraint §74).

positional.description: Stimulus identity (string) or 'none' for explicit opt-out

duration.description: Brief stimulus duration in seconds. Not valid when positional is 'none'.

stimulus_classes.description: Stimulus class definitions for arbitrary MTS. Each class is a named set of stimuli. Defines the class function κ that maps stimuli to class membership.

positional.description: Shorthand: number of classes (class labels auto-generated)

stimulus_classes.required_if_note: Required when MTS type=arbitrary (linter W4). Forbidden context: type=identity triggers linter W3.

errors.STIMULUS_CLASSES_EMPTY_CLASS: Each class must contain at least 1 stimulus
errors.STIMULUS_CLASSES_DUPLICATE_STIMULUS: Same stimulus identity in multiple classes

stimulus_classes.reference: Sidman, M., & Tailby, W. (1982). Conditional discrimination vs. matching to sample: An expansion of the testing paradigm. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 37(1), 5-22. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1982.37-5

training.description: Training phase configuration for MTS. Specifies which stimulus relations to train and mastery criteria. R-3 adds `block_size` for explicit block size declaration (Arntzen 2012: modal 18–36 trials per block in stimulus equivalence research).

criterion.description: Mastery criterion as percent correct (e.g., 90). Saunders & Green (1999) report 90% as the modal criterion in stimulus-equivalence research.

consecutive_blocks.description: Number of consecutive blocks meeting criterion required. Saunders & Green (1999): 2 consecutive blocks is the modal value.

block_size.description: Trials per block used for the mastery criterion evaluation (added in R-3). Arntzen (2012) reports modal values 18–36 in stimulus equivalence research. When omitted, runtime defines block size per implementation or defers to `@session(block_size=...)`.

errors.TRAINING_EMPTY_RELATIONS: relations must contain at least 1 element
errors.TRAINING_NONPOSITIVE_BLOCK_SIZE: block_size must be a positive integer

warnings.TRAINING_BLOCK_SIZE_CONFLICTS_SESSION: both @training(block_size=...) and @session(block_size=...) specified with different values — runtime must disambiguate

training.reference: Sidman, M. (1971). Reading and auditory-visual equivalences. Journal of Speech and Hearing Research, 14(1), 5-13. https://doi.org/10.1044/jshr.1401.05 ; Saunders, K. J., & Green, G. (1999). A discrimination analysis of training-structure effects on stimulus equivalence outcomes. JEAB, 72(1), 117-137. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1999.72-117 ; Arntzen, E. (2012). Training and testing parameters in formation of stimulus equivalence. European Journal of Behavior Analysis, 13(1), 123-135. https://doi.org/10.1080/15021149.2012.11434412

testing.description: Testing phase configuration for MTS. Specifies which relations to test and probe parameters. R-3 adds `block_size` and `criterion` for symmetry with @training, reflecting Saunders & Green (1999) recommendation that test-phase criteria be explicitly declared (typically 90% correct over 1 block for test trials).

probe_ratio.description: Proportion of probe (unreinforced) trials (1.0 = all probes). Fields et al. (1997): 0.5 is typical when baseline trials remain reinforced.

criterion.description: Test-phase mastery criterion as percent correct (added in R-3). Saunders & Green (1999): typically 90% over 1 block for test trials, distinct from training-phase `criterion` which typically spans multiple consecutive blocks.

block_size.description: Trials per test block (added in R-3). Arntzen (2012): modal 18–36 for equivalence testing. Complements @session(block_size) for schedule-wide declaration.

errors.TESTING_EMPTY_RELATIONS: relations must contain at least 1 element
errors.TESTING_NONPOSITIVE_BLOCK_SIZE: block_size must be a positive integer

testing.reference: Sidman, M., & Tailby, W. (1982). Conditional discrimination vs. matching to sample: An expansion of the testing paradigm. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 37(1), 5-22. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1982.37-5 ; Saunders, K. J., & Green, G. (1999). https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1999.72-117 ; Arntzen, E. (2012). https://doi.org/10.1080/15021149.2012.11434412
